declarations.description:
Metro Button Component

A standard push button implementing the Metro design language with distinctive
visual feedback and interactions. Features include:
- Color inversion on hover (foreground becomes background)
- Accent color variant for primary actions
- Tilt animation effect on pointer interaction
- Keyboard navigation support (Enter/Space activation)
- Full ARIA accessibility support

Use this component for standard button actions in forms, toolbars, or any
interactive UI element that triggers an action when clicked.
declarations.name: MetroButton

cssProperties.description: Text and border color of the button in normal state (default: #fff)
cssProperties.name: --metro-foreground

cssProperties.description: Accent color for primary button variant (default: #0078d4)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent

cssProperties.description: Lighter accent for accent hover state (default: #429ce3)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent-light

cssProperties.description: Darker accent for accent pressed state (default: #005a9e)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent-dark

cssParts.description: The main button element that receives hover/press states
cssParts.name: button

slots.description: Default slot for button content (text, icons, or other elements)

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity, no pointer events, and
are removed from the tab navigation order.

members.name: accent
members.description:
Sets the button to use accent color styling. When present, the button
uses the Metro accent color (blue by default) for the background.
Use this for primary actions to make them visually prominent.

members.name: #handleClick
members.description: Handles click events on the button, preventing interaction when disabled.

members.name: #handleKeydown
members.description: Handles keyboard activation (Enter/Space keys) to trigger button action.

members.name: #handlePointerDown
members.description: Applies pressed state styling when the button is pressed via mouse or touch.

events.description: Fired when the button is clicked or activated via keyboard (Enter/Space)
events.name: click

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity, no pointer events, and
are removed from the tab navigation order.

attributes.name: accent
attributes.description:
Sets the button to use accent color styling. When present, the button
uses the Metro accent color (blue by default) for the background.
Use this for primary actions to make them visually prominent.

declarations.description:
Metro Dropdown Button Component

A button that reveals a dropdown menu when clicked. Supports labeled buttons
with optional icons and configurable menu placement. Items in the dropdown
should use the CSS class "menu-item" on their root element.

Features:
- Labeled button with optional icon
- Configurable dropdown placement (top/bottom)
- Animated chevron rotation when opened
- Menu enter/exit animations
- Click outside to close
- Keyboard navigation support
- ARIA attributes for accessibility (menu, expanded state)

Use for action menus, filter options, or any UI requiring a collapsible list
of selectable items attached to a button trigger.
declarations.name: MetroDropdownButton

cssProperties.description: Border color for dropdown menu (default: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2))
cssProperties.name: --metro-border

cssParts.description: The clickable button area containing label, icon, and chevron
cssParts.name: button-content

slots.description: Default slot for dropdown menu items (use CSS class "menu-item" on items)

members.name: label
members.description: Text label displayed on the button. Shows an empty button if not set.

members.name: icon
members.description: Optional icon displayed before the label. Use the icon name from the Metro icon set.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Also prevents the dropdown from opening and removes from tab order.

members.name: placement
members.description:
Position of the dropdown menu relative to the button.
"bottom" places the menu below the button (default).
"top" places the menu above the button.

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the dropdown menu. When true, the menu is displayed.
Can be set programmatically or via the show()/hide() methods.

members.name: #handleButtonClick
members.description:
Handles button click to toggle the dropdown menu.
Prevents interaction when disabled.

members.name: #handleKeydown
members.description: Handles keyboard activation (Enter/Space) to toggle the dropdown.

members.name: #handlePointerDown
members.description: Applies pressed state styling when the button is pressed.

members.name: #toggleDropdown

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The event that triggered the toggle

members.description: Toggles the dropdown menu open/closed state and dispatches appropriate events.

members.name: #handleDocumentClick
members.description: Handles document click events to close the dropdown when clicking outside.

members.name: #handleItemClick
members.description: Handles item click events in the dropdown menu to close it after selection.

members.name: click
members.description:
Programmatically triggers a click on the dropdown button, toggling the menu.
Does nothing if the button is disabled.

members.name: show
members.description:
Programmatically opens the dropdown menu and dispatches the "show" event.
Does nothing if the button is disabled or already open.

members.name: hide
members.description: Programmatically closes the dropdown menu and dispatches the "hide" event.

events.name: show
events.description: Fired when the dropdown menu opens

events.name: hide
events.description: Fired when the dropdown menu closes

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Text label displayed on the button. Shows an empty button if not set.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional icon displayed before the label. Use the icon name from the Metro icon set.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Also prevents the dropdown from opening and removes from tab order.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
Position of the dropdown menu relative to the button.
"bottom" places the menu below the button (default).
"top" places the menu above the button.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the dropdown menu. When true, the menu is displayed.
Can be set programmatically or via the show()/hide() methods.

declarations.description:
Metro Hyperlink Button Component

A button-styled hyperlink that combines the visual appearance of a Metro button
with the navigation behavior of an anchor element. Ideal for call-to-action
links, navigation buttons, or any UI element that needs to both look like a
button and navigate to another page.

Features:
- Styled as a transparent button with accent-colored text
- Supports external links with target="_blank" (adds security attributes)
- Hover underline effect for clear link affordance
- Tilt animation effect on pointer interaction
- Keyboard activation support (Enter/Space)
- Can function as a button without href (uses click handler instead)
declarations.name: MetroHyperlinkButton

cssProperties.description: Text color for the hyperlink button (default: #0078d4)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent

cssParts.description: The main anchor element that receives hover/press states
cssParts.name: button

slots.description: Default slot for button content (text, icons, or other elements)

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity, no pointer events, and
are removed from the tab navigation order.

members.name: href
members.description:
The URL to navigate to when the button is clicked. If not provided,
the button behaves as a regular button without navigation.

members.name: target
members.description:
Specifies where to open the linked document. Use "_blank" for new tab.
When "_blank" is used, security attributes (noopener, noreferrer) are added.

members.name: #handleClick
members.description: Handles click events, preventing interaction when disabled and managing navigation.

members.name: #handleKeydown
members.description: Handles keyboard activation (Enter/Space keys) to trigger navigation or click.

members.name: #handlePointerDown
members.description: Applies pressed state styling when the button is pressed via mouse or touch.

events.description: Fired when the button is clicked or activated via keyboard
events.name: click

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity, no pointer events, and
are removed from the tab navigation order.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
The URL to navigate to when the button is clicked. If not provided,
the button behaves as a regular button without navigation.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description:
Specifies where to open the linked document. Use "_blank" for new tab.
When "_blank" is used, security attributes (noopener, noreferrer) are added.

declarations.description:
Metro Repeat Button Component

A specialized button that repeatedly fires click events while being held down.
Ideal for increment/decrement controls, volume adjustment, or any action that
should repeat when the user holds the button.

Features:
- Configurable initial delay before repeat starts (default: 500ms)
- Configurable repeat interval between clicks (default: 100ms)
- Color inversion on hover
- Accent color variant for primary actions
- Tilt animation effect on pointer interaction
- Keyboard activation support
- Automatically stops repeating when released or on pointer leave

Use for media controls (volume, seek), numeric inputs (spinner buttons),
or any action that benefits from rapid repetition.
declarations.name: MetroRepeatButton

cssProperties.description: Background color in normal state (default: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1))
cssProperties.name: --metro-highlight

cssProperties.description: Accent color for primary button variant (default: #0078d4)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent

cssProperties.description: Lighter accent for accent hover state (default: #429ce3)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent-light

cssProperties.description: Darker accent for accent pressed state (default: #005a9e)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent-dark

cssParts.description: The main button element that receives hover/press states
cssParts.name: button

slots.description: Default slot for button content (text, icons, or other elements)

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity and no pointer events.

members.name: accent
members.description:
Sets the button to use accent color styling for primary actions.
Uses the Metro accent color (blue by default) for the background.

members.name: delay
members.description:
Delay in milliseconds before repeat mode starts after initial press.
Lower values make the button repeat sooner. Only applies to pointer (mouse/touch) events.

members.name: interval
members.description:
Interval in milliseconds between repeated click events during hold.
Lower values make the button repeat faster.

members.name: #handleClick
members.description: Handles click events on the button, preventing interaction when disabled.

members.name: #handleKeydown
members.description:
Handles keyboard activation (Enter/Space keys) to trigger button action.
Note: Keyboard activation does not trigger repeat mode.

members.name: #handlePointerDown
members.description:
Handles pointer down events to start the repeat timer and apply pressed state.
The button will begin repeating click events after the delay period.

members.name: #handlePointerUp
members.description:
Handles pointer up events to stop the repeat timer and remove pressed state.
Called on mouseup, touchend, and mouseleave to ensure repeat always stops.

events.description: Fired initially on press and repeatedly during hold
events.name: click

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
When true, the button is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity and no pointer events.

attributes.name: accent
attributes.description:
Sets the button to use accent color styling for primary actions.
Uses the Metro accent color (blue by default) for the background.

attributes.name: delay
attributes.description:
Delay in milliseconds before repeat mode starts after initial press.
Lower values make the button repeat sooner. Only applies to pointer (mouse/touch) events.

attributes.name: interval
attributes.description:
Interval in milliseconds between repeated click events during hold.
Lower values make the button repeat faster.

declarations.name: updateAriaDisabled

parameters.name: el
parameters.description: The element to update ARIA attributes on

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the element should be marked as disabled

declarations.description:
Updates ARIA attributes for disabled state on an element.
Sets aria-disabled="true" and tabindex="-1" when disabled,
removes aria-disabled and sets tabindex="0" when enabled.

declarations.name: handleDisabledClick

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the element is currently disabled

declarations.description:
Prevents click event propagation when the element is disabled.
Stops immediate propagation and prevents default behavior.

declarations.name: handleKeyboardActivation

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the element is currently disabled

parameters.name: activate
parameters.description: Callback function to execute on activation

declarations.description:
Handles keyboard activation for Enter and Space keys.
Triggers the activation callback when Enter or Space is pressed
and the element is not disabled.

declarations.name: addPressedState

parameters.name: el
parameters.description: The element to apply the pressed state to

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the element is currently disabled (skips if true)

declarations.description:
Adds a temporary "pressed" CSS class to an element.
The class is automatically removed after 50ms to create
a brief visual pressed state effect.

declarations.name: startRepeat

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The RepeatState object to track timers

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Milliseconds to wait before starting repeat interval

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function to call on each activation

declarations.description:
Starts the repeat mode for a button.
Immediately triggers one callback, then waits for the delay
before starting interval-based repeated callbacks.

declarations.name: stopRepeat

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The RepeatState object tracking the timers to clear

declarations.description:
Stops the repeat mode and clears all timers.
Clears both the initial delay timer and the repeat interval.

type.text: Array<{ day: number; month: number; year: number; isCurrentMonth: boolean }>

declarations.description:
Metro Content Dialog Component

A customizable modal dialog for displaying content with optional title,
content area, and button slot. Features smooth enter/exit animations and
backdrop dimming. Suitable for forms, confirmations, or any custom content.

Features:
- Optional title header with border separator
- Scrollable content area for long content
- Dedicated button slot for action buttons
- Optional close button (can be disabled for required dialogs)
- Smooth enter/exit animations
- Backdrop click to close (when closable)
- ARIA dialog role with modal support
- Programmatic show/hide API

Use for custom modal content that requires user interaction or attention.
For simple message dialogs, consider MetroMessageDialog instead.
declarations.name: MetroContentDialog

slots.description: Default slot for dialog content
Slot for action buttons displayed in the footer
slots.name: buttons

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the dialog. When true, the dialog is displayed
with enter animation. Use show() and hide() methods for proper event dispatching.

members.name: closing
members.description:
Set internally during exit animation. When true, the dialog is visible
but fading out. Do not set directly.

members.name: title
members.description:
Title text displayed in the dialog header. If not provided, no header
is rendered.

members.name: closable
members.description:
When true, displays a close button in the top-right corner and allows
closing via backdrop click. When false, the dialog can only be closed
programmatically.

members.name: #handleAnimationEnd
members.description: Handles animation end events to fully close the dialog after exit animation completes.

members.name: #handleBackdropClick
members.description: Handles backdrop click to close the dialog when closable is true.

members.name: #close
members.description: Initiates the close sequence with exit animation and dispatches close event.

members.name: show
members.description: Opens the dialog with enter animation and dispatches show event.

members.name: hide
members.description: Closes the dialog with exit animation and dispatches close event.

events.name: close
events.description: Fired when the dialog closes (bubbles: true)

events.name: show
events.description: Fired when the dialog opens (bubbles: true)

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the dialog. When true, the dialog is displayed
with enter animation. Use show() and hide() methods for proper event dispatching.

attributes.name: closing
attributes.description:
Set internally during exit animation. When true, the dialog is visible
but fading out. Do not set directly.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
Title text displayed in the dialog header. If not provided, no header
is rendered.

attributes.name: closable
attributes.description:
When true, displays a close button in the top-right corner and allows
closing via backdrop click. When false, the dialog can only be closed
programmatically.

declarations.description:
Metro Flyout Component

A floating popup panel that appears relative to a target element.
Features a 3D perspective animation and backdrop overlay. The Flyout
is positioned programmatically and is ideal for contextual menus,
tooltips, or any popup content.

Features:
- 3D perspective enter animation (rotates from -15deg to 0deg)
- Four placement options: top, bottom, left, right
- Backdrop overlay for modal-like behavior
- Automatic positioning based on target element
- Fixed positioning to appear above other content

Unlike MetroTooltip, the Flyout requires a target element and is
designed for more substantial content like menus or forms.
declarations.name: MetroFlyout

slots.description: Default slot for flyout content

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the flyout. When true, the flyout is displayed
with enter animation and positioned relative to the target.

members.name: placement
members.description: Position of the flyout relative to the target element.

members.name: show

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The element to position the flyout relative to

members.description: Shows the flyout positioned relative to the target element.

members.name: hide
members.description: Hides the flyout and dispatches the close event.

members.name: #positionFlyout
members.description: Calculates and sets the flyout position based on placement and target.

members.name: #close
members.description: Handles backdrop click to close the flyout.

events.name: show
events.description: Fired when the flyout opens (bubbles: true)

events.name: close
events.description: Fired when the flyout closes (bubbles: true)

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the flyout. When true, the flyout is displayed
with enter animation and positioned relative to the target.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Position of the flyout relative to the target element.

declarations.description:
Metro Message Dialog Component

A simplified modal dialog designed for displaying messages with optional
title and content. Smaller than ContentDialog, making it ideal for alerts,
confirmations, and simple notifications. Always closable via backdrop click.

Features:
- Compact design optimized for simple messages
- Optional title header with border separator
- Content area for message text
- Dedicated button slot for action buttons (OK, Cancel, etc.)
- Smooth enter/exit animations
- Backdrop click to close
- ARIA dialog role with modal support
- Programmatic show/hide API

Use for simple alert messages, confirmations, or any brief notification
that requires user acknowledgment. For complex content or forms, use
MetroContentDialog instead.
declarations.name: MetroMessageDialog

slots.description: Default slot for dialog message content
Slot for action buttons displayed in the footer
slots.name: buttons

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the dialog. When true, the dialog is displayed
with enter animation. Use show() and hide() methods for proper event dispatching.

members.name: closing
members.description:
Set internally during exit animation. When true, the dialog is visible
but fading out. Do not set directly.

members.name: title
members.description:
Title text displayed in the dialog header. If not provided, no header
is rendered.

members.name: #handleAnimationEnd
members.description: Handles animation end events to fully close the dialog after exit animation completes.

members.name: #close
members.description: Initiates the close sequence with exit animation and dispatches close event.

members.name: show
members.description: Opens the dialog with enter animation and dispatches show event.

members.name: hide
members.description: Closes the dialog with exit animation and dispatches close event.

events.name: close
events.description: Fired when the dialog closes (bubbles: true)

events.name: show
events.description: Fired when the dialog opens (bubbles: true)

Controls the visibility of the dialog. When true, the dialog is displayed
with enter animation. Use show() and hide() methods for proper event dispatching.

attributes.name: closing

Set internally during exit animation. When true, the dialog is visible
but fading out. Do not set directly.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
Title text displayed in the dialog header. If not provided, no header
is rendered.

declarations.description:
Metro Settings Flyout Component

A specialized flyout panel designed for application settings. Slides in
from the right side of the screen with a backdrop overlay. Features focus
management, keyboard support (Escape to close), and restore focus on close.

Features:
- Slides in from right side of viewport
- Two width options: narrow (346px) and wide (646px)
- Backdrop overlay with click-to-close
- Title header with close button
- Focus management (traps focus, restores on close)
- Keyboard support (Escape key closes)
- Smooth slide animations

Use for application settings, preferences, or any side panel that
requires user attention and should be dismissible.
declarations.name: MetroSettingsFlyout

cssProperties.description: Border color for header separator (default: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1))
cssProperties.name: --metro-border

cssParts.description: The header section with title and close button
cssParts.name: flyout-header

slots.description: Default slot for settings content

members.name: title
members.description: Title text displayed in the flyout header.

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the flyout. When true, the panel slides in
from the right and the backdrop appears.

members.name: width
members.description:
Width variant of the flyout.
- "narrow": 346px width (default)
- "wide": 646px width

members.name: #onOpen
members.description: Called when the flyout opens. Stores previous focus and sets focus to close button.

members.name: #onClose
members.description: Called when the flyout closes. Removes keyboard listener and restores focus.

members.name: #handleBackdropClick
members.description: Handles backdrop click to close the flyout.

members.name: #handleClose
members.description: Handles close button click to close the flyout.

members.name: #handleEscapeKey
members.description: Handles Escape key press to close the flyout.

members.name: show
members.description: Opens the flyout, dispatching the "open" event.

members.name: hide
members.description: Closes the flyout, dispatching the "close" event.

events.name: open
events.description: Fired when the flyout opens (bubbles: true, composed: true)

events.name: close
events.description: Fired when the flyout closes (bubbles: true, composed: true)

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Title text displayed in the flyout header.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the flyout. When true, the panel slides in
from the right and the backdrop appears.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description:
Width variant of the flyout.
- "narrow": 346px width (default)
- "wide": 646px width

declarations.description:
Metro Check Box Component

A form-associated checkbox component with Metro styling. Features a custom
visual design with animated checkmark and accent color fill. Implements the
ElementInternals API for form participation.

Features:
- Custom checkbox design with animated checkmark
- Accent color fill for checked state
- Form association via ElementInternals API
- Disabled state support
- Name and value attributes for form submission
- Custom change event with checked state
- ARIA checkbox role support
- Form reset callback support
- Form disabled callback support

Use for boolean options that users can toggle on/off, such as
preferences, agreements, or feature selections.
declarations.name: MetroCheckBox

cssProperties.description: Checked state background and border color (default: #0078d4)
cssProperties.name: --metro-accent

slots.description: Default slot for checkbox label text

members.name: checked
members.description:
The checked state of the checkbox.
When true, the checkbox displays a checkmark and accent fill.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the checkbox is disabled and cannot be toggled.

members.name: name
members.description: Name attribute for form submission.

members.name: value
members.description: Value submitted with the form when checked.

members.name: #updateFormValue
members.description:
Updates the internal form value based on checked state.
Sets value when checked, null when unchecked.

members.name: #toggle
members.description:
Toggles the checked state and dispatches change event.
Does nothing if the checkbox is disabled.

members.name: formDisabledCallback

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the form is now disabled

members.description: Called when the form is disabled/enabled.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Unchecks the checkbox.

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the checked state changes (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { checked: boolean }

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description:
The checked state of the checkbox.
When true, the checkbox displays a checkmark and accent fill.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the checkbox is disabled and cannot be toggled.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name attribute for form submission.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value submitted with the form when checked.

declarations.description:
Metro Password Box Component

A form-associated password input with a reveal button to toggle between
masked and plain text display. Implements the ElementInternals API for
form participation.

Features:
- Password masking with toggle button to reveal text
- Metro icon integration for eye/eye-off icons
- Form association via ElementInternals API
- Optional label support
- Placeholder support (defaults to "Password")
- Disabled state support
- Required validation support
- Custom input and change events
- Form reset and disabled callbacks

Use for password entry fields where users may want to verify their input
by temporarily revealing the text.
declarations.name: MetroPasswordBox

cssParts.description: The input wrapper containing input and button
cssParts.name: input-wrapper

members.name: value
members.description: The current value of the password input.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text displayed when the input is empty.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the input is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

members.name: label
members.description: Label text displayed above the input field.

members.name: name
members.description: Name attribute for form submission.

members.name: required
members.description: When true, the input is required for form submission.

members.name: revealed
members.description:
Internal state tracking whether the password is revealed.
Toggles input type between "password" and "text".

members.name: #updateFormValue
members.description: Updates the internal form value for form submission.

members.name: #handleInput
members.description: Handles input events, updating the value and dispatching custom input event.

members.name: #handleChange
members.description: Handles change events, updating the value and dispatching custom change event.

members.name: #toggleReveal
members.description: Toggles the password reveal state between masked and plain text.

members.name: formDisabledCallback

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the form is now disabled

members.description: Called when the form is disabled/enabled.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Clears the value to empty string.

events.name: input
events.description: Fired when the input value changes (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { value: string }

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the input loses focus and value changed (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { value: string }

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the password input.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text displayed when the input is empty.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the input is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label text displayed above the input field.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name attribute for form submission.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: When true, the input is required for form submission.

attributes.name: revealed
attributes.description:
Internal state tracking whether the password is revealed.
Toggles input type between "password" and "text".

declarations.description:
Metro Slider Component

A form-associated range slider component with Metro styling. Features a custom
track with fill indicator and draggable thumb. Implements the ElementInternals
API for form participation.

Features:
- Custom slider with accent-colored fill track
- Draggable thumb with pointer cursor
- Configurable min, max, and step values
- Form association via ElementInternals API
- Disabled state support
- Custom change event with value detail
- Form reset callback support
- Form disabled callback support

Use for selecting numeric values within a range, such as volume control,
brightness adjustment, or any continuous or stepped value selection.
declarations.name: MetroSlider

cssParts.description: The filled portion of the track
cssParts.name: fill

members.name: value
members.description: The current value of the slider.

members.name: min
members.description: Minimum value of the slider range.

members.name: max
members.description: Maximum value of the slider range.

members.name: step
members.description: Step increment for the slider value.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the slider is disabled and cannot be dragged.

members.name: name
members.description: Name attribute for form submission.

members.name: #updateFormValue
members.description: Updates the internal form value for form submission.

members.name: #handleInput
members.description:
Handles input events from the native range input.
Updates the value and dispatches change event.

members.name: formDisabledCallback

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the form is now disabled

members.description: Called when the form is disabled/enabled.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Resets value to minimum.

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the slider value changes (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { value: number }

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the slider.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: Minimum value of the slider range.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: Maximum value of the slider range.

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: Step increment for the slider value.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the slider is disabled and cannot be dragged.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name attribute for form submission.

declarations.description:
Metro Text Box Component

A form-associated text input component with optional label support.
Implements the ElementInternals API for form participation, allowing
seamless integration with HTML forms.

Features:
- Form association via ElementInternals API
- Optional label that appears above the input
- Placeholder support
- Required validation support
- Disabled state support
- Custom input and change events with value detail
- Form reset callback support
- Form disabled callback support

Use for standard text input fields in forms or standalone text entry.
declarations.name: MetroTextBox

members.name: value
members.description:
The current value of the text input.
Updated on both input and change events.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text displayed when the input is empty.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the input is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

members.name: label
members.description:
Label text displayed above the input field.
If not provided, no label is shown.

members.name: name
members.description: Name attribute for form submission.

members.name: required
members.description: When true, the input is required for form submission.

members.name: #updateFormValue
members.description: Updates the internal form value for form submission.

members.name: #handleInput
members.description: Handles input events, updating the value and dispatching custom input event.

members.name: #handleChange
members.description: Handles change events, updating the value and dispatching custom change event.

members.name: formAssociatedCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Clears the value.

members.name: formDisabledCallback

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the form is now disabled

members.description: Called when the form is disabled/enabled.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Clears the value to empty string.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called when form state is restored (e.g., from browser autocomplete).

events.name: input
events.description: Fired when the input value changes (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { value: string }

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the input loses focus and value changed (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { value: string }

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The current value of the text input.
Updated on both input and change events.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text displayed when the input is empty.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the input is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Label text displayed above the input field.
If not provided, no label is shown.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name attribute for form submission.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: When true, the input is required for form submission.

declarations.description:
Metro Toggle Switch Component

A form-associated toggle switch component with Metro styling. Features a sliding
thumb animation and accent color fill. Implements the ElementInternals API for
form participation. Similar in function to a checkbox but with a switch metaphor.

Features:
- Custom switch design with sliding thumb animation
- Accent color fill for "on" state
- Form association via ElementInternals API
- Disabled state support
- Name and value attributes for form submission
- Custom change event with on state
- ARIA switch role support
- Form reset callback support
- Form disabled callback support

Use for binary on/off settings where the switch metaphor is more appropriate
than a checkbox, such as enabling/disabling features or toggling modes.
declarations.name: MetroToggleSwitch

cssProperties.description: Easing curve for color transitions (default: cubic-bezier(0.1, 0.9, 0.2, 1))
cssProperties.name: --metro-easing

slots.description: Default slot for switch label text

members.name: on
members.description:
The on/off state of the switch.
When true, the switch is in the "on" position with accent color.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the switch is disabled and cannot be toggled.

members.name: name
members.description: Name attribute for form submission.

members.name: value
members.description: Value submitted with the form when on.

members.name: #updateFormValue
members.description:
Updates the internal form value based on on state.
Sets value when on, null when off.

members.name: #toggle
members.description:
Toggles the on state and dispatches change event.
Does nothing if the switch is disabled.

members.name: formDisabledCallback

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether the form is now disabled

members.description: Called when the form is disabled/enabled.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called when the form is reset. Turns the switch off.

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the on state changes (bubbles: true, composed: true) Detail: { on: boolean }

attributes.name: on
attributes.description:
The on/off state of the switch.
When true, the switch is in the "on" position with accent color.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the switch is disabled and cannot be toggled.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name attribute for form submission.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value submitted with the form when on.

declarations.description:
Metro App Bar Button Component

A specialized button designed for use within the Metro App Bar. Features a circular
icon container with hover inversion effects and optional label display. The label
appears only when the parent App Bar is in expanded state.

Features:
- Circular icon container with border
- Hover color inversion effect (icon becomes dark on light background)
- Optional text label that appears when app bar is expanded
- Support for both regular app bar and menu item modes
- Metro icon integration with size variants

Use this component within metro-app-bar for navigation and action buttons.
For menu items in the expandable panel, use the "menu-item" attribute.
declarations.name: MetroAppBarButton

cssParts.description: The circular container for the icon
cssParts.name: icon-circle

slots.description: Default slot for custom content (used when no icon is specified)

members.name: icon
members.description:
The name of the Metro icon to display. See metro-icon for available icons.
If not provided, the default slot content is rendered instead.

members.name: label
members.description:
Text label displayed below the icon. Only visible when the parent
metro-app-bar has the "expanded" attribute set.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description:
The name of the Metro icon to display. See metro-icon for available icons.
If not provided, the default slot content is rendered instead.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Text label displayed below the icon. Only visible when the parent
metro-app-bar has the "expanded" attribute set.

declarations.description:
Metro App Bar Separator Component

A visual separator line for use within the Metro App Bar. Creates a
vertical line between buttons or groups of buttons to visually
distinguish different sections of the app bar.

Features:
- Vertical line separator with standard dimensions
- Configurable visibility (can be hidden while preserving layout)
- Standard spacing margins on both sides
- Semi-transparent styling to blend with Metro design

Use within metro-app-bar to separate groups of related buttons.
declarations.name: MetroAppBarSeparator

cssProperties.description: Foreground color for the separator line (default: #ffffff)
cssProperties.name: --metro-foreground

cssProperties.description: Spacing on both sides of the separator (default: 8px)
cssProperties.name: --metro-spacing-sm

members.name: visible
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the separator. When false, the separator
is hidden but still occupies space in the layout.

attributes.name: visible
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the separator. When false, the separator
is hidden but still occupies space in the layout.

declarations.description:
Metro App Bar Toggle Button Component

A toggle button for use within the Metro App Bar. Functions as a checkbox
with visual checked state using accent color fill. Supports the same
circular icon design as the standard AppBarButton.

Features:
- Toggle/checkbox behavior with checked state
- Circular icon container with accent fill when checked
- Hover color inversion effect
- Optional text label that appears when app bar is expanded
- Support for both regular and menu item modes
- ARIA checkbox role support

Use within metro-app-bar for toggle actions like pinning, favoriting,
or any on/off state that needs to be visually prominent.
declarations.name: MetroAppBarToggleButton

cssParts.description: The circular container for the icon
cssParts.name: icon-circle

slots.description: Default slot for custom content (used when no icon is specified)

members.name: icon
members.description: The name of the Metro icon to display. See metro-icon for available icons.

members.name: label
members.description:
Text label displayed below the icon. Only visible when the parent
metro-app-bar has the "expanded" attribute set.

members.name: checked
members.description:
The checked/toggled state of the button. When true, the icon circle
is filled with the accent color.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the button is disabled and cannot be toggled.

members.name: menuItem
members.description:
When true, renders the button in menu item mode (row layout with
smaller icon and always-visible label). Set automatically when
used in the app bar's menu panel.

members.name: #handleClick
members.description: Handles click events to toggle the checked state.

members.name: #handleKeyDown
members.description: Handles keyboard events (Enter/Space) to toggle the checked state.

members.name: #setupObserver
members.description: Sets up a mutation observer to watch the parent app bar's expanded attribute.

members.name: #updateExpandedState
members.description: Updates the data-expanded attribute based on parent app bar state.

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the checked state changes. Detail: { checked: boolean }

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: The name of the Metro icon to display. See metro-icon for available icons.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Text label displayed below the icon. Only visible when the parent
metro-app-bar has the "expanded" attribute set.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description:
The checked/toggled state of the button. When true, the icon circle
is filled with the accent color.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When true, the button is disabled and cannot be toggled.

attributes.name: menu-item
attributes.description:
When true, renders the button in menu item mode (row layout with
smaller icon and always-visible label). Set automatically when
used in the app bar's menu panel.

declarations.description:
Metro App Bar Component

A fixed-position application bar that provides primary navigation and actions.
The App Bar appears at the top or bottom of the screen and supports an expandable
menu panel for additional options. It's designed for mobile and touch-first interfaces.

Features:
- Fixed positioning at top or bottom of viewport
- Expandable menu panel with slide animation
- Slots for buttons, content, and menu items
- Visual separator between content and button areas
- Smooth height transitions when expanding/collapsing

The App Bar is commonly used as the primary navigation container in Metro-style
applications, housing navigation buttons, action buttons, and an overflow menu.
declarations.name: MetroAppBar

cssProperties.description: Background color of the app bar (default: #1f1f1f)
cssProperties.name: --metro-background

cssProperties.description: Slow transition duration for height changes (default: 333ms)
cssProperties.name: --metro-transition-slow

cssParts.description: The main content area of the app bar
cssParts.name: app-bar-content

cssParts.description: The "..." button that toggles the menu panel
cssParts.name: ellipsis-btn

slots.description: Default slot for app bar buttons (displayed in the button area)
Slot for content displayed on the left side of the bar
slots.name: content

slots.description: Slot for menu items displayed in the expandable panel
slots.name: menu

members.name: placement
members.description:
Position of the app bar. "bottom" places it at the bottom of the viewport (default),
"top" places it at the top. Affects border placement and animation direction.

members.name: expanded
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the expandable menu panel.
When true, the menu panel is displayed with slide-in animation.

members.name: #toggleMenu
members.description:
Toggles the expandable menu panel open or closed.
Updates the expanded property and dispatches appropriate events.

events.description: Fired when the menu panel is expanded (composed: true, bubbles: true)
events.name: expanded

events.description: Fired when the menu panel is collapsed (composed: true, bubbles: true)
events.name: collapsed

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
Position of the app bar. "bottom" places it at the bottom of the viewport (default),
"top" places it at the top. Affects border placement and animation direction.

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the expandable menu panel.
When true, the menu panel is displayed with slide-in animation.

declarations.description:
Metro Hub Section Component

A child component for MetroHub that represents a single horizontally-scrolling
section. Each section has an optional clickable header and a content area
for hosting tiles, lists, or other content.

Features:
- Optional clickable section header with hover accent color
- Large typography-focused header styling
- Flexible content area with standard padding
- Designed to be used within metro-hub for horizontal scrolling layouts

Use as children of metro-hub. Set the "header" attribute to define the
section title. The header is clickable and changes to accent color on hover.
declarations.name: MetroHubSection

slots.description: Default slot for section content (tiles, lists, etc.)

members.name: header
members.description:
Text displayed as the section header.
If not provided, no header is rendered.

attributes.name: header
attributes.description:
Text displayed as the section header.
If not provided, no header is rendered.

declarations.description:
Metro Hub Component

A horizontal scrolling container that groups related sections with headers.
The Hub is designed for organizing large amounts of related content into
distinct, independently scrollable sections. Similar to the Windows Phone Hub concept.

Features:
- Optional main title displayed above the section container
- Horizontal scrolling for sections with hidden scrollbar
- Large, typography-focused section headers
- Flexible section sizing with minimum widths
- Touch-friendly horizontal scrolling

Use the Hub when you need to present multiple distinct content sections
that users can horizontally navigate between. Combine with metro-hub-section
children to create individual sections.
declarations.name: MetroHub

cssProperties.description: Font size for the main title (default: 42px)
cssProperties.name: --metro-font-size-xxlarge

slots.description: Default slot for metro-hub-section children

members.name: title
members.description:
Main title displayed at the top of the hub.
If not provided, no title is rendered.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
Main title displayed at the top of the hub.
If not provided, no title is rendered.

declarations.description:
Metro Panorama Item Component

A child component for MetroPanorama that represents a single scrollable panel.
Each PanoramaItem is displayed at 85% width with snap-point alignment,
creating a peek-at-next-item effect that encourages horizontal exploration.

Features:
- Optional header displayed in accent color
- Semi-transparent background for visual distinction
- Flexbox layout with scrollable content area
- Designed to be used within metro-panorama for immersive scrolling

Use as children of metro-panorama. Set the "header" attribute to define
the panel title displayed at the top.
declarations.name: MetroPanoramaItem

slots.description: Default slot for panel content

members.name: header
members.description:
Text displayed as the panel header in accent color.
If not provided, no header is rendered.

attributes.name: header
attributes.description:
Text displayed as the panel header in accent color.
If not provided, no header is rendered.

declarations.description:
Metro Panorama Component

A full-screen, horizontally scrolling view with parallax background effect.
The Panorama is inspired by the Windows Phone Panorama control, providing
an immersive browsing experience with smooth touch-based scrolling and
momentum-based physics.

Features:
- Full-width horizontal scrolling with momentum/inertia
- Parallax background image effect
- Touch and pointer gesture support with physics-based scrolling
- Spring-back boundary animation when scrolling past edges
- Snap points for each panorama item
- Smooth scroll behavior with optional scroll-snap

The Panorama is ideal for showcasing featured content, image galleries,
or any full-screen horizontally-navigable content.
declarations.name: MetroPanorama

slots.description: Default slot for metro-panorama-item children

members.name: title
members.description: Optional title displayed at the top of the panorama.

members.name: backgroundImage
members.description:
URL of the background image for the parallax effect.
The image is displayed at 15% opacity and moves at 20% of scroll speed.

members.name: #handleScroll
members.description:
Handles scroll events to update the parallax background position.
The background moves at 20% of the scroll speed for a subtle effect.

members.name: #handlePointerDown
members.description:
Handles pointer down events to begin gesture tracking.
Only responds to left mouse button or touch pointers.

members.name: #handlePointerMove
members.description:
Handles pointer move events for gesture-based scrolling.
Updates scroll position based on drag delta with boundary compression.

members.name: #handlePointerUp

parameters.name: _e
parameters.description: The pointer event (unused but required for signature)

members.description:
Handles pointer up events to end gestures and apply inertia.
Calculates velocity and applies momentum-based scrolling.

members.name: #runSpringBack

parameters.name: overscroll
parameters.description: The amount scrolled past the boundary (negative for left, positive for right)

members.description:
Runs a spring-back animation when scrolling past the container boundaries.
Animates the container back to the valid scroll range with spring physics.

members.name: #stopAnimation
members.description: Stops any running animation frame for inertia or spring-back.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Optional title displayed at the top of the panorama.

attributes.name: background-image
attributes.description:
URL of the background image for the parallax effect.
The image is displayed at 15% opacity and moves at 20% of scroll speed.

declarations.description:
Metro Pivot Item Component

A child component for MetroPivot that represents a single tab panel.
Each PivotItem contains content that is displayed when its corresponding
tab header is selected in the parent Pivot component.

Features:
- Header text attribute for the tab label
- Automatic active state management by parent Pivot
- Content padding and box-sizing for consistent layout
- Smooth opacity and scale transitions when becoming active

Use as children of metro-pivot. Set the "header" attribute to define
the tab label text.
declarations.name: MetroPivotItem

slots.description: Default slot for the pivot item content

members.name: header
members.description:
Text displayed in the pivot tab header.
If not set, a default "Item N" label is generated by the parent.

attributes.name: header
attributes.description:
Text displayed in the pivot tab header.
If not set, a default "Item N" label is generated by the parent.

declarations.description:
Metro Pivot Component

A tab-based navigation component that allows users to switch between different
content sections. Features large, typography-focused headers with smooth animated
transitions between content panes. The Pivot is ideal for organizing related
content into separate views within a single screen.

Features:
- Large typography-based tab headers with scaling animation
- Horizontal slide animation between content sections
- Scrollable tab list for overflow scenarios
- Active tab indicator (accent-colored underline)
- Smooth opacity and scale transitions for content
- Keyboard and ARIA accessibility support

Use Pivot when you need to organize related content into separate but
equally important views, such as different sections of a profile or
different views of a data set.
declarations.name: MetroPivot

cssProperties.description: Border color for tab underline (default: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2))
cssProperties.name: --metro-border

slots.description: Default slot for metro-pivot-item children

members.name: selectedIndex
members.description:
Index of the currently selected pivot item (0-based).
Changing this updates the visible content and active tab styling.

members.name: #selectItem

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The zero-based index of the item to select

members.description:
Selects a pivot item by index and updates the view.
Dispatches a selectionchanged event with the new index.

events.name: selectionchanged
events.description: Fired when the selected tab changes. Detail: { selectedIndex: number }

attributes.name: selectedIndex
attributes.description:
Index of the currently selected pivot item (0-based).
Changing this updates the visible content and active tab styling.

declarations.description:
Metro Split View Component

A navigation component that divides the screen into a pane and content area.
Supports multiple display modes including overlay (slides over content),
inline (pushes content), and compact (shrunken pane that expands).

Features:
- Three display modes: overlay, inline, compact
- Configurable pane placement (left or right)
- Smooth slide/width animations when opening/closing
- Backdrop overlay in overlay mode
- Backdrop click to close in overlay mode
- Programmatic API for show/hide/toggle

Use SplitView for master-detail layouts, navigation drawers, or any UI
that requires a collapsible side panel alongside main content.
declarations.name: MetroSplitView

cssProperties.description: Background color for pane and content (default: #1f1f1f)
cssProperties.name: --metro-background

cssProperties.description: Border color for pane separator (default: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2))
cssProperties.name: --metro-border

cssParts.description: The content area within the pane
cssParts.name: pane-content

slots.description: Slot for navigation/content in the side pane
slots.name: pane

slots.description: Default slot for main content area

members.name: open
members.description:
Controls the visibility of the pane. When true, the pane is displayed.
In overlay mode, this also shows the backdrop.

members.name: displayMode
members.description:
Display mode for the pane:
- "overlay": Pane slides over content (default)
- "inline": Pane pushes content (width animation)
- "compact": Pane shows as narrow strip, expands on open

members.name: panePlacement
members.description:
Placement of the pane:
- "left": Pane on the left side (default)
- "right": Pane on the right side

members.name: #handleBackdropClick
members.description: Handles backdrop click in overlay mode to close the pane.

members.name: toggle
members.description: Toggles the pane open/closed state and dispatches appropriate event.

members.name: show
members.description: Opens the pane if closed and dispatches the "paneopened" event.

members.name: hide
members.description: Closes the pane if open and dispatches the "paneclosed" event.

events.name: paneclosed
events.description: Fired when the pane closes (bubbles: true)

events.name: paneopened
events.description: Fired when the pane opens (bubbles: true)

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Controls the visibility of the pane. When true, the pane is displayed.
In overlay mode, this also shows the backdrop.

attributes.name: display-mode
attributes.description:
Display mode for the pane:
- "overlay": Pane slides over content (default)
- "inline": Pane pushes content (width animation)
- "compact": Pane shows as narrow strip, expands on open

attributes.name: pane-placement
attributes.description:
Placement of the pane:
- "left": Pane on the left side (default)
- "right": Pane on the right side

type.text: { top: number; right: number; bottom: number; left: number }
{ top: number; right: number; bottom: number; left: number }

declarations.description:
Metro Expander Component

A collapsible content container with a clickable header. Features smooth
height animation when expanding/collapsing and a rotating chevron icon
to indicate state.

Features:
- Clickable header to toggle expanded state
- Smooth height transition animation (max-height based)
- Rotating chevron icon indicating state
- Optional title attribute or slot for header content
- Highlight background on header hover
- ARIA expanded state support

Use for collapsible sections of content, FAQ-style layouts, or anywhere
you need to show/hide content while preserving layout space.
declarations.name: MetroExpander

slots.description: Default slot for collapsible content
Slot for custom header content (used when title attribute not set)
slots.name: header

members.name: expanded
members.description:
Controls whether the expander is open (expanded) or closed (collapsed).
When true, the content area is visible with height animation.

members.name: title
members.description:
Title text displayed in the header. If not provided, the "header" slot
is used instead for custom header content.

members.name: #toggle
members.description: Toggles the expanded state and dispatches the expanded event.

events.name: expanded
events.description: Fired when the expander state changes Detail: { expanded: boolean }

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Controls whether the expander is open (expanded) or closed (collapsed).
When true, the content area is visible with height animation.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
Title text displayed in the header. If not provided, the "header" slot
is used instead for custom header content.

declarations.description:
Metro Toast Component

A notification container that displays toast messages at the top of the screen.
Supports multiple severity levels with distinct colors, auto-dismiss, and manual close.

Features:
- Four severity levels: informational, success, warning, error
- Auto-dismiss with configurable duration
- Manual close button on each toast
- Slide-down enter and slide-up exit animations
- Color-coded border and icon for each severity
- Stacking multiple toasts vertically
- Clear all functionality

The component is typically used via the exported showToast() function for
simple global toast notifications, or instantiated directly for more control.
declarations.name: MetroToast

cssParts.description: Content wrapper for title and message
cssParts.name: toast-content

members.name: show
members.description: Shows a new toast notification.

members.name: hide
members.description: Hides a specific toast by ID with exit animation.

members.name: clearAll
members.description: Hides all currently displayed toasts.

events.description: Fired when a toast is displayed
events.name: toastshown

events.description: Fired when a toast is dismissed
events.name: toashidden

declarations.name: showToast
declarations.description:
Shows a toast notification using the global toast instance.
Creates the global instance on first call.

declarations.name: hideToast
declarations.description: Hides a specific toast by ID from the global toast instance.

Metro Tooltip Component

A floating informational popup that appears near a target element to provide
additional context or explanation. Features automatic positioning, delayed
show for hover interactions, and fade animations.

Features:
- Four placement options: top, bottom, left, right
- Automatic positioning based on target element
- Fade in/out transitions
- Delayed show support for hover scenarios
- Immediate hide support
- Pointer-events none to allow clicking through
- Fixed positioning for viewport-relative placement

Unlike MetroFlyout, the Tooltip is designed for brief informational text
and does not support rich content or interaction. Use for explaining icons,
buttons, or other UI elements.
declarations.name: MetroTooltip

slots.description: Default slot for tooltip content (text or HTML)

members.name: open
members.description: Controls the visibility of the tooltip. When true, the tooltip is displayed.

members.name: text
members.description:
Text content displayed in the tooltip.
Can be set via attribute or slot for more complex content.

members.name: placement
members.description: Position of the tooltip relative to the target element.

members.name: show

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The element to position the tooltip relative to

members.description: Shows the tooltip positioned relative to the target element.

members.name: hide
members.description: Hides the tooltip immediately, clearing any pending delayed show.

members.name: showDelayed

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The element to position the tooltip relative to

members.description: Shows the tooltip after a delay. Useful for hover interactions.

members.name: hideImmediate
members.description: Hides the tooltip immediately and cancels any pending delayed show.

members.name: #positionTooltip
members.description: Calculates and sets the tooltip position based on placement and target.
